meta.kicker: PRIVATE AGENT FACTORY · ORACLE E-BUSINESS SUITE
meta.headline: AP Invoice Automation Agent
meta.subhead: Invoice-to-Match for EBS — extract, 3-way match, and code supplier invoices straight into the Payables Open Interface, validated against your live EBS data.

problem.title: Manual AP is slow, costly, and error-prone

cards.desc: fully-loaded cost to process one invoice by hand
typical cycle time — late payments, missed discounts
of spend lost to duplicate and over-billed invoices

problem.note: AP automation is a $3B+ category with multiple unicorns. Every EBS shop runs tens of thousands to millions of invoices a year — and pays incumbents $3–8 each.

solution.title: An EBS-native agent that does the AP grunt work

cards.title: Reads any invoice
cards.desc: PDF, email or text — a deterministic parser (zero external deps) with an optional LLM for messy/scanned docs.

cards.title: Checks live EBS
cards.desc: Supplier, PO, receipts, tax and GL — read-only, org-scoped lookups against the real E-Business Suite.

cards.title: Matches & codes
cards.desc: Deterministic 3-way match with price/qty tolerances, GL distribution inheritance and tax classification.

cards.title: Validates & delivers
cards.desc: A QA gate proves every invoice balances before it writes balanced AP Open Interface files.

solution.note: Same agentic pattern as Oracle's Private Agent Factory contract-renewal blog — pointed at the far larger AP revenue pool.

architecture.title: EBS 19c owns the data + logic; PAF + managed MCP do the rest
architecture.subhead: Agentic AP automation on OCI · EBS 19c system of record · managed MCP

tiers.title: PAF — Agent Builder
tiers.subtitle: on ADB 26ai sidecar
items: NO EBS DATA · not in the data path

architecture.flow: document → PAF LLM extract → match_code_and_validate (XXPAF) → balanced AP interface rows → Payables Open Interface Import
architecture.flow_note: Read/validate via MCP · writes loaded by the standard import · deterministic logic + QA in EBS

controls.title: Built for auditors, not just speed

cards.title: Read-only by design
cards.desc: Only SELECTs against EBS; the single write path is off by default and touches interface tables only.

cards.title: Multi-org safe
cards.desc: Org-scoped queries + FND_GLOBAL.APPS_INITIALIZE context; bind variables everywhere — no injection surface.

cards.title: Deterministic policy
cards.desc: Match, tolerance and coding rules live outside the LLM — reproducible and explainable for SOX.

cards.title: QA gate before load
cards.desc: Every invoice is checked to balance, reconcile and resolve all FKs; failures are held, never imported.

controls.note: Every interface row is stamped with the agent run-id and a confidence score — traceable end to end.

proof.kicker: PROVEN ON LIVE EBS VISION DATA
proof.title: Real invoice. Real PO. Clean match.
proof.scenario_sub: Advanced Network Devices · Org 204 · Oracle EBS 19c Vision

rows.b: invoiced vs received on every line
GL distribution inherited from the PO
invoice total — header balances to lines

proof.caption: Verified against the live database during the build — not a mock.

why_us.title: The partner to put this into production

cards.title: Deep EBS expertise
cards.desc: Decades running Oracle E-Business Suite for enterprise customers — we know your AP, PO and GL setup.

cards.title: Reference build in hand
cards.desc: A working, QA'd agent proven against live Vision data — not slideware. Ready to point at your instance.

cards.title: OCI + PAF aligned
cards.desc: Positioned for the Private Agent Factory motion on AI Database 26ai, with TCO and licensing mapped.

next_steps.title: Let's run it on your EBS instance

steps.title: Discovery
steps.desc: Confirm AP volumes, invoice mix and target operating unit.

steps.title: Proof of value
steps.desc: Point the agent at a read-only EBS copy; demo a live 3-way match.

steps.title: Productionize
steps.desc: Deploy on PAF / 26ai with AME approvals and scheduled import.

callout.label: Agent list price
callout.multiple_note: return on the agent fee — with margin to share with partners.

bom.notes: no broker VM; no Instant Client host

estimator.note: Managed services = run hours × blended rate + OCI run + agent license; 12/24/36-mo at 0/10/20% discount. OCI = list, License Included; BYOL ~76% lower on the DB.

tech_design.intro: EBS 19c (OCI DBaaS/Exadata) is the system of record and the home of all logic — the XXPAF_AP_AGENT PL/SQL package. PAF runs on an ADB 26ai sidecar holding zero EBS data and is not in the data path. The OCI Database Tools Managed MCP (HTTPS + OAuth/IAM) exposes XXPAF as a governed custom tool + SQL Reports over a Database Tools Connection — no broker, no DB link, no thick-mode in production.
processing: Extraction in the PAF LLM node → invoice JSON
match_code_and_validate (XXPAF): supplier resolve, duplicate check, 3-way match (dual %/$ tolerance), GL/tax coding, QA gate
Balanced AP interface rows (header = Σ lines incl. TAX line)
Load via the standard Payables Open Interface Import (writes off the MCP path)
tech_design.assurance: Deterministic logic in PL/SQL; the Python reference engine is the test oracle (parity test asserts Python == PL/SQL on golden PO 3495 → balanced $632,500.00, QA PASS).

prereqs: Oracle Instant Client (thick mode, NNE) for dev/test only
Access to the target EBS 19c (OCI DBaaS/Exadata)
For production: OCI Database Tools + Managed MCP enabled (no local client needed)

steps.title: Install (dev/test)
steps.body: Outputs land in output/: the interface CSVs, agent_trace.json and qa_report.json.

steps.title: Deploy the EBS logic
steps.body: Compile the XXPAF package into EBS 19c (system of record + logic).

steps.title: Import the agent + wire PAF (production)
steps.body: Import the .paf (PAF → My Custom Flows → Import; password simple4u), register the OCI managed MCP, rebind the MCP node + LLM, then run the validation gate.

sow.overview: Syntax will implement the EBS AP Invoice Automation Agent on Oracle Private Agent Factory, integrating to the customer's EBS 19c via the OCI Database Tools Managed MCP, with deterministic 3-way match, GL/tax coding, QA, and load through the standard Payables Open Interface Import.
deliverables: XXPAF PL/SQL package deployed to EBS 19c
PAF agent (.paf import + canvas) + managed-MCP tool registration
QA pack (tests + QA_REPORT) and technical design document
sow.acceptance: a green live golden test plus a balanced interface load in customer UAT
sow.legal_note: This generic SOW shell is subject to the parties' MSA; have counsel review before use.

flow_diagram.title: How the agent works — functional flow
flow_diagram.subhead: Document → extract → governed lookups → deterministic policy → QA → interface

steps.detail: PAF LLM node → structured invoice JSON
governed EBS reads: supplier · PO · receipts · tax · GL (MCP)
balance · reconcile · resolve FKs — PASS / HOLD
balanced AP interface rows → Open Interface Import

branch.label: Exception / HOLD
branch.detail: routed to human review (AME approval) — never auto-loaded

governance: KPI / monitoring dashboard (throughput, STP %, exception rate) via the published REST API
Exception-review console for HOLD / needs-approval invoices (human-in-the-loop)
Model & prompt management — versioned prompts, model pinning, regression checks
Periodic accuracy review — re-run the validation gate on a sampled set each quarter
Change & incident management — controlled prompt/tool changes; severity-based incident handling
Quarterly value review — realized savings vs the ROI model; tuning backlog

phases.name: Discovery
phases.outcomes: Confirm volumes, invoice mix, target OU; finalize spec.yaml; access + Database Tools Connection

phases.name: Build
phases.outcomes: XXPAF PL/SQL + managed-MCP tools; agent flowGraph; deliverables generated

phases.name: Validate
phases.outcomes: Validation gate on ground-truth set; Python↔PL/SQL parity; zero unsafe actions

phases.name: Go-live
phases.outcomes: Import .paf, rebind MCP + LLM, UAT, scheduled Open Interface Import

phases.name: Hypercare
phases.outcomes: Monitored run, exception triage, KT; transition to managed services

entitlements: 1 production agent (this domain); add-on agents priced separately
Prompt / model / tool updates + PAF-release compatibility
Installation, technical design, validation gate, QA report

golden_record.notes: vendor 21 Advanced Network Devices, 4 lines, fully received, $632,500.00
